---
species: "Ascophyllum nodosum"
accepted_code: "ASNO"
alternate_codes: ["ASDI"]
diminutive: ""
alternate_diminutives: ["", ""]
common_name: "knotted wrack"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
date: ""
date-format: " "
description: "Long, green/brown algae typically found in the upper rocky intertidal with distinct unpaired air bladders"
image: '/header_images/ascophyllum_nodosum.jpg'
categories:
  - brown algae
sampling_programs:
    - keen_upc
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_bruv
    - lsw_quad
habitat:
  - intertidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80300901
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 145541
---

![](/header_images/ascophyllum_nodosum.jpg)
Photo from Misjel Decleer via [WORMS](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=image&pic=34379&tid=145541)

**Other images:** <!-- Other images with attribution -->
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** It forms long fronds that hang down, typically from hard substrate such as boulders. The fronds have a unpaured airbladders, with a new one generating each year.
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** Closely related to Fucus species. 

<br/>

**Extended Description:** <!-- Extended description -->

<!-- link to worms page -->
